# Cairo

Cairo is an open-source event tracking and customer data platform (CDP). It collects events from any app or AI agent, then routes them to analytics tools, databases, Slack, and 15+ other destinations.

## Install

```bash
npm install @cairo/tracker
```

## Quick Start

```typescript
import { Cairo } from '@cairo/tracker';

const cairo = Cairo.init({
  writeKey: 'your-write-key',
  host: 'https://your-cairo-instance.com',
});

// Track an event
cairo.track({
  event: 'signup',
  userId: 'user_123',
  properties: { plan: 'free', source: 'landing_page' },
});

// Identify a user
cairo.identify({
  userId: 'user_123',
  traits: { name: 'Jane', email: 'jane@example.com', plan: 'free' },
});

// Track a page view
cairo.page({ name: 'Dashboard', userId: 'user_123' });

// Associate user with a group/org
cairo.group({
  groupId: 'org_456',
  userId: 'user_123',
  traits: { name: 'Acme Inc', plan: 'enterprise' },
});

// Link anonymous user to known user
cairo.alias({ userId: 'user_123', previousId: 'anon_abc' });

// Before process exits
await cairo.shutdown();
```

## HTTP API (no SDK needed)

All events go to `/v2/*`. Authenticate with `X-Write-Key` header.

### POST /v2/track

```json
{
  "event": "purchase",
  "userId": "user_123",
  "properties": { "amount": 29.99, "currency": "USD" },
  "timestamp": "2026-03-20T10:00:00Z"
}
```

### POST /v2/identify

```json
{
  "userId": "user_123",
  "traits": { "name": "Jane", "email": "jane@example.com" }
}
```

### POST /v2/batch

Send multiple events in one request:

```json
{
  "batch": [
    { "type": "track", "event": "page_viewed", "userId": "user_123", "properties": { "page": "/home" } },
    { "type": "track", "event": "button_clicked", "userId": "user_123", "properties": { "button": "cta" } }
  ]
}
```

### POST /v2/page

```json
{ "name": "Pricing", "userId": "user_123" }
```

### POST /v2/group

```json
{ "groupId": "org_456", "userId": "user_123", "traits": { "name": "Acme" } }
```

### POST /v2/alias

```json
{ "userId": "user_123", "previousId": "anon_abc" }
```

## AI Agent Tracking

For tracking AI agent behavior (LLM calls, tool usage, decisions), use `@cairo/agent-tracker`:

```bash
npm install @cairo/agent-tracker
```

```typescript
import { AgentTracker } from '@cairo/agent-tracker';

const tracker = AgentTracker.init({
  writeKey: 'your-write-key',
  host: 'https://your-cairo-instance.com',
  agentId: 'my-agent',
});

tracker.generation({ model: 'gpt-4o', promptTokens: 500, completionTokens: 200, latencyMs: 1200 });
tracker.toolCall({ tool: 'search', input: { q: 'hello' }, success: true, latencyMs: 300 });
tracker.decision({ type: 'routing', options: ['a', 'b'], chosen: 'a', confidence: 0.9 });
tracker.error({ type: 'timeout', message: 'API timed out', recoverable: true });

await tracker.shutdown();
```

## Config Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| writeKey | (required) | Authentication key |
| host | http://localhost:8080 | Cairo server URL |
| flushAt | 20 | Events before auto-flush |
| flushInterval | 5000 | Ms between flushes |
| maxRetries | 3 | Retry attempts |
| timeout | 10000 | Request timeout (ms) |
| debug | false | Log to console |
| enable | true | Set false to disable |

## Self-hosting

Cairo is a Node.js server backed by PostgreSQL:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/outcome-driven-studio/cairo.git
cd cairo
npm install
# Set POSTGRES_URL in .env
npm start
```

Server runs on port 8080 by default.

## Key Features

- Segment-compatible API (drop-in replacement)
- Identity resolution (merge anonymous + known users)
- Event transformations (rename, filter, enrich before delivery)
- Tracking plans (schema validation for events)
- 15+ destinations (Mixpanel, BigQuery, Slack, webhooks, etc.)
- GDPR compliance (user deletion, suppression, audit log)
- Event replay (backfill new destinations with historical data)
- AI query (ask questions about your data in plain English)
